verses.text: God reassures Jacob His servant: 'I will pour water upon him that is thirsty... I will pour my spirit upon thy seed,' and many shall own His name. The Spirit revives. God promises an outpouring that makes the barren fruitful and claims new hearts.

'I am the first, and I am the last; and beside me there is no God,' who alone foretells; 'is there a God beside me?... I know not any.' He is utterly unique. The God who declares the future is the only God there is.

'I am the first, and I am the last; and beside me there is no God,' who alone foretells; 'is there a God beside me?... I know not any.' He is utterly unique. The God who declares the future is the only God there is.

'I am the first, and I am the last; and beside me there is no God,' who alone foretells; 'is there a God beside me?... I know not any.' He is utterly unique. The God who declares the future is the only God there is.
Those who make graven images are vanity, and their cherished idols cannot profit; their makers shall be ashamed. Idolatry is folly. To worship the work of one's own hands is to embrace emptiness.

Those who make graven images are vanity, and their cherished idols cannot profit; their makers shall be ashamed. Idolatry is folly. To worship the work of one's own hands is to embrace emptiness.

The smith and carpenter labour over their idol, growing weary, faint, and hungry as they shape a god. The maker is greater than the made. How absurd that a tired, hungry man should fashion something to worship.

A man burns half a log for warmth and bakes his bread, then of the rest 'maketh a god... and saith, Deliver me; for thou art my god.' The contradiction is glaring. He prays to a block from the same wood that cooked his meal.

They neither know nor consider, for God has shut their eyes; such a one 'feedeth on ashes' and cannot ask, 'Is there not a lie in my right hand?' Sin blinds the idolater. He cannot see the falsehood he clutches.

'Remember these, O Jacob'; God has blotted out their sins, so the heavens are called to sing, for 'the LORD hath done it.' Redemption brings rejoicing. Pardoned sin is cause for all creation to praise.

The LORD who made all things and frustrates the diviners names Cyrus beforehand: 'He is my shepherd, and shall perform all my pleasure,' to rebuild Jerusalem. Prophecy is exact. God can name a deliverer generations before his birth.
